facts: Honey bees communicate with each other through the 'waggle dance' which tells other bees the location of flowers.
A single bee colony can contain up to 60,000 bees working together.
Bees visit about 50-100 flowers during a single collecting trip.
One pound of honey requires about 2 pounds of honey bees to gather the nectar.
A bee's wings beat approximately 200 times per second.
Honey bees can fly at speeds up to 20 mph.
The average worker bee lives about 6 weeks during busy seasons.
A queen bee can lay up to 2,000 eggs per day.
Bees have five eyes - three simple eyes on top and two large compound eyes in front.
Honey never spoils and can be preserved indefinitely.
Bees are responsible for pollinating about 1/3 of the food we eat.
A bee must visit about 2 million flowers to make 1 pound of honey.
Bees see ultraviolet light that humans cannot see.
The temperature inside a beehive is regulated at about 95°F or 35°C.
Bees perform a 'funeral service' by carrying dead bees out of the hive.
Honey contains all the vitamins and minerals needed to sustain human life.
A worker bee produces about 1/12th of a teaspoon of honey in her lifetime.
Bees have been around for over 130 million years.
Queen bees lay about 1.5 million eggs in their lifetime.
The hexagon shape of honeycomb is the most efficient structure in nature.
Bees can recognize human faces.
Propolis, made by bees, has antibacterial and antifungal properties.
A bee's memory is remarkable - they can remember colors, shapes, and scents.
Bees navigate using the sun as a compass.
The honey bee is the state insect of 17 U.S. states.
